verdict.rationale: (provided by user) Accept default-off/operator-controlled security posture and existing substring-test convention.

prior_orchestrator_verdict.rationale: Implementation is functionally correct and satisfies all 6 acceptance criteria: the flag gates window.close() (default False preserves auto-close), postMessage and the diagnostics beacon still fire before the gate, window_should_close stays truthful (!CALLBACK_KEEP_TAB_OPEN), the error/no-opener branches are untouched, and the Python bool is safely lowered to JS. Two items block a clean auto-approve. CR-001 (security, decision_needed): with the flag on, the callback tab stays open indefinitely with the OAuth code/state still in its URL/history — a real, if mild (values are already consumed server-side), exposure tradeoff on auth code with no prod-guard; a human should accept the default-off/operator-controlled posture or require a safeguard. CR-002 (test strength, patch): the new tests assert served-script substrings only and never execute the JS, so a branch-body inversion could pass green. 1 low/informational finding dismissed (reused close message; other-branch completeness — the error/no-opener paths never close, so the flag is correctly irrelevant there). Confidence low: a decision_needed finding remains.

business_review.note: CALLBACK_KEEP_TAB_OPEN gates window.close() in the success+opener branch.

business_review.criterion: 2. Config-gated, default OFF, default behavior unchanged
business_review.note: MCP_AUTH_CALLBACK_KEEP_TAB_OPEN: bool = False; default script still calls window.close().

business_review.criterion: 3. Kept-open tab shows a user-facing message
4. postMessage + beacon still fire; window_should_close truthful
business_review.note: Both fire unconditionally before the gate; window_should_close: !CALLBACK_KEEP_TAB_OPEN.
Error branch has no window.close() and no flag reference; unchanged.

business_review.criterion: 6. Tests cover default value, default-closes, flag-on-keeps-open
business_review.note: All three tests present and passing (77 bridge+feature-gating tests green).

standards_review.notes: Commit subject 'EPMCDME-11515: Add config flag...' matches EPMCDME-####: Description.
ruff check + format --check clean on all three changed files.
OAuth code/state URL persistence when flag enabled — raised as CR-001.

findings.title: Kept-open callback tab prolongs OAuth code/state exposure in URL/history
findings.problem: When MCP_AUTH_CALLBACK_KEEP_TAB_OPEN=true, the success branch no longer closes the tab, so the callback URL (carrying the OAuth code/state query params) remains in the address bar and browser history indefinitely. No production guard and no URL scrub (history.replaceState) accompany the flag.
findings.impact: Extended visibility of OAuth callback parameters (screen shares, history, physical access). Mitigated by: default-off/opt-in, and the code/state are single-use and already consumed server-side by the time the bridge renders.
findings.recommendation: Human decision: (a) accept as-is (default-off, operator-controlled, diagnostic-only), or (b) add a safeguard — scrub the URL via history.replaceState before leaving the tab open, and/or warn/refuse when the flag is enabled outside a diagnostic environment.

findings.title: New tests assert served-script substrings only; cannot catch a branch-body inversion
findings.problem: The two script tests check for literal substrings in the served JS and never execute it. The const-value flip (= true; / = false;) is asserted, but a semantic inversion (e.g. swapping the if/else bodies) would keep all substrings present and ship green.
findings.impact: A future edit that inverts the gate logic would not be caught by the suite. Consistent with the file's existing substring-only convention, so this is a pre-existing test-strength limitation, not a regression.
findings.recommendation: Cheaply strengthen the keep-open test to assert the gated structure precisely (e.g. that the else-branch updateMessage follows the if (!CALLBACK_KEEP_TAB_OPEN) block), or accept the file's existing substring convention.
